atoms.content: An ML Engineer must be proficient in software engineering, distributed systems, cloud architecture, and have a solid foundation in statistics and linear algebra.
A successful ML Engineer is like a decathlete: they don't just run or jump; they must balance math, coding, and server management simultaneously.

1. Deploying models to production (MLOps).
2. Monitoring data drift and model degradation.
3. Writing efficient, scalable Python code.
Is building the theoretical math behind a new algorithm a core responsibility of an ML Engineer? (Answer: No, that is typically a Machine Learning Researcher).
Essential tools include Python, Scikit-Learn, TensorFlow or PyTorch, SQL for data extraction, and cloud platforms like AWS SageMaker or Google Vertex AI.
The majority of an ML Engineer's time is spent on data preprocessing and feature engineering — not building models. Clean data is the foundation of accurate predictions.
